title: fix(network): forward memory context in validation stream calls

relatedIssues.title: Network validation calls crash with ObservationalMemory when scope: 'thread'

summary: Network validation stream calls (runDefaultCompletionCheck, generateFinalResult, generateStructuredFinalResult) were not forwarding memory context to agent.stream(), causing crashes when ObservationalMemory is configured with scope: 'thread'. This PR adds a getValidationMemoryOptions() helper that extracts thread/resource IDs from CompletionContext (with fallback to runId/networkName) and passes them to all three validation stream calls with readOnly: true and workingMemory disabled to avoid side effects during validation.
maintainerNotes: Clean, well-scoped bug fix from an external contributor. The fix addresses issue #13651 (labeled impact:high, sourced from Discord). Changes are minimal and surgical: one new helper function and three call sites updated. Includes comprehensive tests covering explicit IDs, fallback behavior, and structured output paths. CodeRabbit review passed with only a minor nitpick suggesting one additional fallback test for generateStructuredFinalResult. CI checks are still pending (tests queued behind setup-actions-with-secrets gates, which is expected for external contributor PRs). Vercel deploy failures are expected (authorization required for external PRs). No merge conflicts. Changeset included.
